mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off sentiment and negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) during labor market shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand growth, which sustains corporate earnings momentum and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline, or 60-day forward returns are negative in over 55% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the industrial production index during a late-cycle expansion indicates capacity constraints and supply chain bottlenecks, which compress profit margins and force negative equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: The signal predicts positive returns during late-cycle phases, or the negative correlation with 20-day returns disappears (Rank IC > -0.01) when the economy enters recession.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL reading dropping below 235.0 signals disinflationary pressure that removes rate hike fears, prompting immediate risk-on positioning and positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are negative in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the threshold loses predictive accuracy (Rank IC < 0.02) over a 2-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects a strengthening labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate revenue, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between housing starts and retail sales, where housing starts decline while retail sales rise, indicates a shift from durable goods to services that disrupts capital expenditure cycles, causing negative equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative returns, or 20-day forward returns are positive in over 55% of divergence periods during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production index reading exceeding 105.0 signals robust manufacturing output that exceeds baseline expectations, triggering immediate algorithmic buying and positive S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 5-day returns in more than 65% of triggered instances, or the signal's Rank IC drops below 0.01 over a 2-year rolling evaluation.
